audit_status.source_base: Hebrew Exodus / Shemot sanctuary instruction text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: First-pass draft reviewed for incense altar, ransom/census silver, bronze basin, anointing oil, incense blend, holiness restrictions.

verses.restored: You shall make altar for burning incense; of acacia wood you shall make it.
A cubit its length and a cubit its width, square it shall be, and two cubits its height; its horns from it.
Overlay it with pure gold, its top, sides all around, and horns; make for it gold molding all around.
Two gold rings make for it under molding on two sides; make on two sides, as housings for poles to carry it.
Make poles of acacia wood and overlay them with gold.
Put it before veil that is over ark of testimony, before cover over testimony, where I meet with you.
Aaron shall burn on it fragrant incense morning by morning; when he dresses lamps, he shall burn it.
And when Aaron raises lamps between evenings, he shall burn it, continual incense before YHWH throughout generations.
You shall not offer upon it strange incense, burnt offering, grain offering, and drink offering you shall not pour on it.

Aaron shall make atonement on its horns once in year with blood/life of sin/misalignment offering of atonements; once in year he shall make atonement upon it throughout generations. Holy of Holies it is to YHWH.
YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,
When you lift head of children of Israel according to their counted ones, each shall give ransom/covering of one's soul/life to YHWH when counting them, so no plague be among them when counting.
This they shall give, everyone passing over to counted ones: half shekel by shekel of sanctuary, twenty gerahs the shekel; half shekel as contribution to YHWH.
Everyone passing over to counted ones, from son of twenty years and upward, shall give contribution of YHWH.
The rich shall not increase and poor shall not diminish from half shekel, to give contribution of YHWH to make atonement over your souls/lives.
Take silver of atonements from children of Israel and give it for service of tent of meeting; it shall be for children of Israel as memorial before YHWH, to make atonement over your souls/lives.
YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,
Make bronze basin and bronze stand for washing; put it between tent of meeting and altar, and put water there.
Aaron and his sons shall wash from it their hands and their feet.
When they enter tent of meeting, they shall wash with water so they do not die; or when they approach altar to minister, to turn smoke of fire-offering to YHWH.
They shall wash their hands and feet so they do not die; it shall be age-long statute for them, for him and his seed throughout generations.
YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,
You take for yourself chief spices: flowing myrrh five hundred, fragrant cinnamon half of it, two hundred fifty, fragrant cane two hundred fifty,
cassia five hundred by sanctuary shekel, and olive oil a hin.
Make it holy anointing oil, perfume mixture, work of perfumer; holy anointing oil it shall be.
Anoint with it tent of meeting and ark of testimony,
table and all its vessels, lampstand and its vessels, incense altar,
altar of burnt offering and all its vessels, basin and its stand.
Sanctify them, and they shall be Holy of Holies; all touching them shall be holy.
Aaron and his sons you shall anoint, and sanctify them to serve as priests to me.
To children of Israel speak, saying, Holy anointing oil this shall be to me throughout your generations.
Upon flesh of human it shall not be poured, and by its composition you shall not make like it; holy it is, holy it shall be to you.
Whoever mixes like it or puts from it on stranger shall be cut off from one's people.
YHWH said to Moses, Take for yourself fragrant spices: stacte, onycha, galbanum, fragrant spices with pure frankincense; equal part to equal part shall it be.
Make it incense, perfume mixture, work of perfumer, salted, pure, holy.
Beat some of it very fine, and put some before testimony in tent of meeting where I meet with you; Holy of Holies it shall be to you.
The incense you make, by its composition you shall not make for yourselves; holy to YHWH it shall be to you.
Whoever makes like it to smell it shall be cut off from one's people.

translation_notes: Incense altar is tied to lamp tending morning/evening: fragrance and light rhythms are linked.
Census silver is equal for rich and poor; life-value before YHWH is not scaled by wealth.
Basin washing marks priestly approach with bodily preparation and ritual safety.
Anointing oil/incense restrictions protect holy mixtures from ordinary/private appropriation.
safety_note: Do not use census/ransom language to imply richer lives are worth more; the text explicitly forbids rich increasing or poor decreasing the half-shekel.
